---
title: "MLSys·im"
page-layout: custom
sidebar: false
title-block-style: none
include-after-body:
  - text: |
      <script src="styles/landing.js" defer></script>
---
<!-- ============================================================
     HERO — tight: title, subtitle, install, CTAs, trust bar
     ============================================================ -->

::: {.im-hero}
::: {.im-hero-inner}

::: {.im-title}
MLSys·im
:::

::: {.im-subtitle}
Build intuition for ML system performance, cost, and carbon — from first principles.
:::

::: {.im-install}
<code class="im-cmd">pip install mlsysim</code>
<button class="im-copy-btn" id="copy-btn" onclick="copyInstall()">Copy</button>
:::

::: {.im-ctas}
[Get Started](getting-started.qmd){.im-btn .im-btn-primary}
[Tutorials](tutorials/index.qmd){.im-btn .im-btn-ghost}
:::

```{=html}
<div class="im-trust-bar">
  <span>Companion to <a href="https://mlsysbook.ai">Harvard CS 249r</a></span>
  <span class="im-trust-sep">·</span>
  <span>Python 3.10+</span>
  <span class="im-trust-sep">·</span>
  <span>No GPU required</span>
  <span class="im-trust-sep">·</span>
  <span><a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/cs249r_book">GitHub</a></span>
  <span class="im-trust-sep">·</span>
  <span>CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0</span>
</div>
```

:::
:::

<!-- ============================================================
     SEE IT IN ACTION — code left, output right
     ============================================================ -->

```{=html}
<div class="im-hero im-hero-showcase">
<div class="im-hero-inner im-hero-split">

  <!-- LEFT: Code input -->
  <div class="im-hero-code">
    <div class="im-code-label">Input</div>
    <pre class="im-code-block"><code><span class="im-kw">import</span> mlsysim
<span class="im-kw">from</span> mlsysim <span class="im-kw">import</span> Engine

profile = Engine.solve(
    model    = mlsysim.Models.ResNet50,
    hardware = mlsysim.Hardware.Cloud.A100,
    batch_size = <span class="im-num">1</span>,
    precision  = <span class="im-str">"fp16"</span>
)</code></pre>
  </div>

  <!-- RIGHT: Output -->
  <div class="im-hero-output">
    <div class="im-code-label">Output</div>
    <pre class="im-output-block"><code><span class="im-out-key">Bottleneck:</span>  <span class="im-out-val im-out-ok">Compute Bound</span>
<span class="im-out-key">Latency:</span>     <span class="im-out-val">0.54 ms</span>
<span class="im-out-key">Throughput:</span>  <span class="im-out-val">1,847 img/s</span>
<span class="im-out-key">MFU:</span>         <span class="im-out-val">4.9%</span>
<span class="im-out-key">Memory:</span>      <span class="im-out-val">0.05 GB / 80 GB</span>
<span class="im-out-key">AI (FLOP/B):</span> <span class="im-out-val">80.1</span>  <span class="im-out-dim">← above ridge point</span></code></pre>
  </div>

</div>
</div>
```

<!-- ============================================================
     CAPABILITIES — 4 static visualization cards
     ============================================================ -->

::: {.im-content}

::: {.im-section}

::: {.im-section-header}
### What it models
:::

```{=html}
<div class="im-cap-grid">

  <div class="im-cap-card">
    <div class="im-cap-viz">
      <svg viewBox="0 0 320 130">
        <line x1="40" y1="100" x2="300" y2="100" stroke="rgba(148,163,184,0.3)" stroke-width="1"/>
        <line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="100" stroke="rgba(148,163,184,0.3)" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="170" y="118" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">Arithmetic Intensity (FLOP/Byte)</text>
        <text x="15" y="60" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" transform="rotate(-90,15,60)">FLOP/s</text>
        <line x1="40" y1="95" x2="160" y2="30" stroke="#38bdf8" stroke-width="2"/>
        <line x1="160" y1="30" x2="300" y2="30" stroke="#38bdf8" stroke-width="2"/>
        <circle cx="90" cy="68" r="5" fill="#f59e0b" opacity="0.9"/>
        <text x="100" y="63" fill="#f59e0b" font-size="8">Memory Bound</text>
        <circle cx="230" cy="30" r="5" fill="#10b981" opacity="0.9"/>
        <text x="240" y="25" fill="#10b981" font-size="8">Compute Bound</text>
        <text x="160" y="45" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="7">Ridge Point</text>
        <line x1="160" y1="30" x2="160" y2="100" stroke="rgba(148,163,184,0.2)" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4,3"/>
      </svg>
    </div>
    <h4>Roofline Analysis</h4>
    <p>Identify whether your workload is memory-bound or compute-bound on any hardware.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="im-cap-card">
    <div class="im-cap-viz">
      <svg viewBox="0 0 320 130">
        <text x="160" y="15" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">Llama-3.1-8B on H100</text>
        <rect x="30" y="30" width="120" height="50" rx="6" fill="rgba(56,189,248,0.08)" stroke="rgba(56,189,248,0.25)" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="90" y="46" fill="#38bdf8" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Pre-fill</text>
        <text x="90" y="64" fill="#7dd3fc" font-size="18" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">4.2 ms</text>
        <text x="90" y="76" fill="#64748b" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle">TTFT (compute-bound)</text>
        <text x="160" y="60" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="14">&#x2192;</text>
        <rect x="180" y="30" width="120" height="50" rx="6" fill="rgba(16,185,129,0.08)" stroke="rgba(16,185,129,0.25)" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="240" y="46" fill="#10b981" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Decode</text>
        <text x="240" y="64" fill="#6ee7b7" font-size="18" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">0.8 ms</text>
        <text x="240" y="76" fill="#64748b" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle">ITL (memory-bound)</text>
        <rect x="70" y="96" width="180" height="22" rx="4" fill="rgba(245,158,11,0.08)" stroke="rgba(245,158,11,0.25)" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="160" y="111" fill="#f59e0b" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">KV-Cache: 2.1 GB / 80 GB available</text>
      </svg>
    </div>
    <h4>LLM Serving</h4>
    <p>Model pre-fill and decode phases, KV-cache pressure, and time-to-first-token.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="im-cap-card">
    <div class="im-cap-viz">
      <svg viewBox="0 0 320 130">
        <line x1="85" y1="10" x2="85" y2="110" stroke="rgba(148,163,184,0.1)" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="80" y="22" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="9" text-anchor="end">Quebec</text>
        <rect x="85" y="12" width="10" height="14" rx="3" fill="#10b981"/>
        <text x="100" y="23" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="8">20 g CO&#x2082;/kWh</text>
        <text x="80" y="47" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="9" text-anchor="end">Norway</text>
        <rect x="85" y="37" width="5" height="14" rx="3" fill="#10b981" opacity="0.8"/>
        <text x="95" y="48" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="8">10 g CO&#x2082;/kWh</text>
        <text x="80" y="72" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="9" text-anchor="end">US Avg</text>
        <rect x="85" y="62" width="95" height="14" rx="3" fill="#f59e0b"/>
        <text x="185" y="73" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="8">390 g CO&#x2082;/kWh</text>
        <text x="80" y="97" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="9" text-anchor="end">Poland</text>
        <rect x="85" y="87" width="200" height="14" rx="3" fill="#ef4444"/>
        <text x="290" y="98" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="8">820 g CO&#x2082;/kWh</text>
      </svg>
    </div>
    <h4>Sustainability</h4>
    <p>Same workload, different region — up to 41× difference in carbon footprint.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="im-cap-card">
    <div class="im-cap-viz">
      <svg viewBox="0 0 320 130">
        <text x="160" y="15" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">256&#xd7; H100 &#x2014; GPT-3 175B</text>
        <rect x="10" y="28" width="95" height="42" rx="6" fill="rgba(124,58,237,0.08)" stroke="rgba(124,58,237,0.25)" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="57.5" y="45" fill="#a78bfa" font-size="8" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Data Parallel</text>
        <text x="57.5" y="62" fill="#c4b5fd" font-size="16" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">32&#xd7;</text>
        <rect x="112.5" y="28" width="95" height="42" rx="6" fill="rgba(56,189,248,0.08)" stroke="rgba(56,189,248,0.25)" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="160" y="45" fill="#38bdf8" font-size="8" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Tensor Parallel</text>
        <text x="160" y="62" fill="#7dd3fc" font-size="16" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">4&#xd7;</text>
        <rect x="215" y="28" width="95" height="42" rx="6" fill="rgba(16,185,129,0.08)" stroke="rgba(16,185,129,0.25)" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="262.5" y="45" fill="#10b981" font-size="8" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Pipeline Parallel</text>
        <text x="262.5" y="62" fill="#6ee7b7" font-size="16" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">2&#xd7;</text>
        <line x1="20" y1="82" x2="300" y2="82" stroke="rgba(148,163,184,0.15)" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="85" y="98" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">Scaling Efficiency</text>
        <text x="85" y="118" fill="#a78bfa" font-size="18" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">74%</text>
        <text x="235" y="98" fill="#94a3b8" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">Pipeline Bubble</text>
        <text x="235" y="114" fill="#f59e0b" font-size="18" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">6.3%</text>
      </svg>
    </div>
    <h4>Distributed Training</h4>
    <p>3D parallelism decomposition with scaling efficiency and pipeline bubble analysis.</p>
  </div>

</div>
```

:::


<!-- WHAT CAN YOU ANSWER? -->
::: {.im-section}

::: {.im-section-header}
### What can you answer?
:::

::: {.im-questions-grid}

::: {.im-question}
Is my workload **memory-bound** or **compute-bound**?
:::

::: {.im-question}
How many GPUs do I need to train a **70B model** in 24 hours?
:::

::: {.im-question}
What is the **carbon footprint** of training in Iowa vs. Quebec?
:::

::: {.im-question}
What is the optimal **checkpoint interval** for a 1000-GPU job?
:::

::: {.im-question}
How much does **quantization** to INT8 actually save in latency?
:::

::: {.im-question}
What is the **3-year TCO** for a 64×H100 cluster?
:::

:::

:::


<!-- SOLVER HIGHLIGHTS -->
::: {.im-section}

::: {.im-section-header}
### Model every constraint in your ML system
:::

<div class="im-stats">
  <div class="im-stat"><span class="im-stat-num">22</span><span class="im-stat-label">Solvers</span></div>
  <div class="im-stat"><span class="im-stat-num">22</span><span class="im-stat-label">System Walls</span></div>
  <div class="im-stat"><span class="im-stat-num">6</span><span class="im-stat-label">Constraint Domains</span></div>
  <div class="im-stat"><span class="im-stat-num">&lt;0.3s</span><span class="im-stat-label">Full Analysis</span></div>
</div>

::: {.im-solvers-grid}

::: {.im-solver-card .im-solver-roofline}
**Roofline Analysis**
Compute vs. memory bottleneck identification using the Iron Law. Single-node latency and throughput.
:::

::: {.im-solver-card .im-solver-serving}
**LLM Serving**
Time-to-first-token (TTFT), inter-token latency (ITL), and KV-cache memory pressure.
:::

::: {.im-solver-card .im-solver-distributed}
**3D Parallelism**
Data, tensor, and pipeline parallel scaling with communication overhead and bubble analysis.
:::

::: {.im-solver-card .im-solver-sustain}
**Sustainability**
Energy, carbon footprint (kg CO₂e), and water usage across datacenter regions.
:::

::: {.im-solver-card .im-solver-tco}
**Total Cost of Ownership**
CapEx, OpEx, electricity, maintenance, and per-query economics over any time horizon.
:::

::: {.im-solver-card .im-solver-reliability}
**Reliability & Queueing**
Fleet MTBF, checkpoint intervals, tail latency (P99), and SLA compliance.
:::

:::

[See the full solver guide →](models-and-solvers.qmd){.im-see-all}

:::


<!-- TUTORIALS -->
::: {.im-section}

::: {.im-section-header}
### Learn by doing
:::

```{=html}
<div class="im-tutorial-grid">
  <a href="tutorials/00_hello_roofline.html" class="im-tutorial-card-link">
    <div class="im-tutorial-card im-card-beginner">
      <span class="im-tutorial-badge im-badge-beginner">Beginner</span>
      <h4>Hello, Roofline</h4>
      <p>Memory-bound vs. compute-bound in 5 lines of Python. Sweep batch sizes and see the roofline crossover.</p>
    </div>
  </a>
  <a href="tutorials/01_memory_wall.html" class="im-tutorial-card-link">
    <div class="im-tutorial-card im-card-beginner">
      <span class="im-tutorial-badge im-badge-beginner">Beginner</span>
      <h4>The Memory Wall</h4>
      <p>Quantify model weights, activations, and optimizer state. Find out why your 7B model won't fit on one GPU.</p>
    </div>
  </a>
  <a href="tutorials/02_two_phases.html" class="im-tutorial-card-link">
    <div class="im-tutorial-card im-card-intermediate">
      <span class="im-tutorial-badge im-badge-intermediate">Intermediate</span>
      <h4>Two Phases, One Request</h4>
      <p>Model the two phases of autoregressive generation (pre-fill and decode) and diagnose KV-cache pressure.</p>
    </div>
  </a>
  <a href="tutorials/05_quantization.html" class="im-tutorial-card-link">
    <div class="im-tutorial-card im-card-intermediate">
      <span class="im-tutorial-badge im-badge-intermediate">Intermediate</span>
      <h4>Quantization Trade-offs</h4>
      <p>INT8 vs. FP16 vs. FP4 — measure the memory savings, throughput gains, and accuracy costs of compression.</p>
    </div>
  </a>
  <a href="tutorials/06_scaling_1000_gpus.html" class="im-tutorial-card-link">
    <div class="im-tutorial-card im-card-advanced">
      <span class="im-tutorial-badge im-badge-advanced">Advanced</span>
      <h4>Scaling to 1000 GPUs</h4>
      <p>Ring all-reduce communication, pipeline bubbles, and scaling efficiency on 256 GPUs.</p>
    </div>
  </a>
  <a href="tutorials/07_geography.html" class="im-tutorial-card-link">
    <div class="im-tutorial-card im-card-advanced">
      <span class="im-tutorial-badge im-badge-advanced">Advanced</span>
      <h4>Geography is a Systems Variable</h4>
      <p>Same model, same GPU, yet up to 41× difference in carbon footprint depending on where you train.</p>
    </div>
  </a>
</div>
```

[See all 12 tutorials →](tutorials/index.qmd){.im-see-all}

:::

:::

<!-- ============================================================
     FOOTER
     ============================================================ -->

```{=html}
<footer class="im-footer">
  <div class="im-footer-inner">
    <div class="im-footer-brand">
      <strong>MLSys·im</strong>
      <span>First-principles ML systems modeling</span>
    </div>
    <div class="im-footer-links">
      <div class="im-footer-col">
        <strong>Docs</strong>
        <a href="getting-started.html">Get Started</a>
        <a href="tutorials/index.html">Tutorials</a>
        <a href="models-and-solvers.html">Solvers</a>
        <a href="architecture.html">Architecture</a>
      </div>
      <div class="im-footer-col">
        <strong>Project</strong>
        <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/cs249r_book">GitHub</a>
        <a href="https://pypi.org/project/mlsysim/">PyPI</a>
        <a href="whitepaper.html">Research Paper</a>
        <a href="contributing.html">Contributing</a>
      </div>
      <div class="im-footer-col">
        <strong>Related</strong>
        <a href="https://mlsysbook.ai">Harvard CS 249r</a>
        <a href="https://mlsysbook.ai">ML Systems Textbook</a>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="im-footer-bottom">
      <span>CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0</span>
      <span class="im-trust-sep">·</span>
      <span>Cite: Reddi, V.J. (2025). <em>MLSys·im: First-Principles Infrastructure Modeling for ML Systems.</em></span>
    </div>
  </div>
</footer>
```
